Author: anurdin01 | Updated: 2026-05-29T10:50:44Z

Task Description

  • Add Validation for 0,0 Coordinates on Publish
  • Show alert danger "The image is in the ocean and Google does not accept images there." at API Alert Info

I've added new danger message on alert info API, so if there has al least one panorama that has geolocation at ocean or null island the message will appear.

I have also addressed this issue in the publication process also

Document docs/v2/user/get-account-statistic.md

[GoThruMedia/GoThru-API-New] get-account-statistic.md

Path: docs/v2/user/get-account-statistic.md

User — Get Account Statistic

GET /users/account-statistic

Auth: middleware('auth') — user_id injected into request context by middleware

Flow:

  1. Request enters → GetAccountStatisticController@handle builds GetAccountStatisticDto from $request->all().
  2. Controller delegates to GetAccountStatisticAction::execute(GetAccountStatisticDto).
  3. Action runs business logic:
    • If $dto->userId === 0, throws UserAccountStatisticNotFoundException (USER_ACCOUNT.STATISTIC_NOT_FOUND).
    • Checks Redis cache key app_account_stats_{userId} (TTL 3600s). On hit, returns decoded JSON directly.
    • On cache miss, calls buildStats($userId):
      • Calls AccountStatisticRepository::isAgencyAdmin($userId) — checks gothru_mod.agency.
      • If agency admin: calls getSubAgentIds($userId) → appends self → calls getPublishedToursInfoForAgency(array $ids) aggregating moderation.audit across all sub-agents + admin.
      • If regular user: calls getPublishedToursInfo($userId) aggregating moderation.audit for the user alone.
      • Calls getGothruPosition($userId) — subquery rank from moderation.audit (users with higher count + 1).
      • Calls getOverlayStatistics($userId) — conditional aggregate from hosted_tours.tours.
    • Stores result in Redis (setex, TTL 3600s).
    • Returns plain array of stats.
  4. Controller receives result → wraps into encrypted JSON response:
    • Success → ResponseEnvelopeHelper::encryptResult(['account_stats' => $accountStats], messages, 200)
    • UserAccountDomainException → UserAccountExceptionHttpMapper::toStatusCode($e) → responseMessages()
    • \Throwable → ResponseEnvelopeHelper::statusCodeFromThrowable($e) → responseMessages()

DTO (app/DTOs/User/Account/GetAccountStatisticDto.php):

FieldTypeSourceNotes
userIdintrequest.client_user_id ?? request.user_idclient_user_id takes priority (agency impersonation flow); injected by auth middleware; cast to int, defaults to 0 if absent

Response (200) — encrypted:

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "messages": "Data retrieved",
    "cry": {
      "c1p": "<base64 AES-256-CBC ciphertext>",
      "f0ur": "<hex IV>",
      "su1fur": "<hex salt>"
    },
    "ls": "2026-05-29T11:17:30.620453+00:00",
    "e4": "en3xk9ab..."
  }
}

Decrypted payload shape:

{
  "account_stats": {
    "tours_published": 12,
    "panos_published": 340,
    "rank": 5,
    "hosted_overlays": 3,
    "google_overlays": 1
  },
  "messages": "Data retrieved"
}

Errors:

domainCodeHTTPCondition
USER_ACCOUNT.STATISTIC_NOT_FOUND404userId resolves to 0 (missing/unauthenticated)
(fallback \Throwable)dynamicResponseEnvelopeHelper::statusCodeFromThrowable()

Usage:

curl -X GET https://api.gothru.co/users/account-statistic \
  -H "Authorization: {token}"

Notes:

  • Redis cache key app_account_stats_{userId} — TTL 3600s. Cache is not invalidated on publish; consumers should account for up to 1-hour staleness.
  • Agency admins (rel_type = 'agency' in agency table) receive aggregated stats across all sub-agents with subscribtion = 4.
  • rank is 0 when the user has zero published tours in audit table.
  • Response is always encrypted (encryptResult) regardless of any request header.
  • Compliance: ~85% ADR. Legacy HelperClass::connectRedis() remains in Action — tracked for future extraction to CacheService.
Document docs/v2/auth/login.md

[GoThruMedia/GoThru-API-New] login.md

Path: docs/v2/auth/login.md

Auth — Login

POST /auth/login

Auth: public (no middleware)

Flow:

  1. Request enters → LoginController@handle builds LoginDto from $request->all().
  2. Controller delegates to LoginAction::execute(LoginDto).
  3. Action runs business logic:
    • If email or password is empty string, throws InvalidCredentialsException (AUTH.INVALID_CREDENTIALS).
    • Encodes password via AuthTokenHelper::encodePassword($dto->password).
    • Calls AuthSessionRepository::findUserByCredentials($dto->email, $encodedPassword) — matches users by user_name + password; if null, throws InvalidCredentialsException.
    • If verified_email === 0 or is_active === 0, throws AccountNotActiveException (AUTH.ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE).
    • Resolves JWT TTL from hardcoded map (test accounts: 300s / 60s; all others: 2592000s / 30 days).
    • Calls AuthTokenHelper::createJwtToken(['email' => $dto->email], $ttl) to produce JWT.
    • Calls AuthSessionRepository::upsertGtToken($userId, $userName, $email) — inserts or updates auth.token_id on gothru_mod.
    • Returns ['token' => string, 'user_id' => int, 'email' => string, 'user_name' => string].
  4. Controller receives result → starts session via SessionClass: start(), register('auth', ['expiry' => time()+86400, 'user_id' => ..., 'session_id' => ...]).
  5. Controller wraps response:
    • Success → ResponseEnvelopeHelper::responseMessages(['token' => ..., 'cookie' => $session->getCookie()], 'auth.login_success', 200)
    • AuthDomainException → AuthExceptionHttpMapper::toStatusCode($e) → responseMessages()
    • \Throwable → ResponseEnvelopeHelper::statusCodeFromThrowable($e) → responseMessages()

DTO (app/DTOs/Auth/LoginDto.php):

FieldTypeSourceNotes
emailstringbody emaildefaults to ''; empty → AUTH.INVALID_CREDENTIALS
passwordstringbody passworddefaults to ''; empty → AUTH.INVALID_CREDENTIALS

Note: repository matches by users.user_name, so email field is actually the username/login identifier.

Response (200):

{
  "statusCode": 200,
  "data": {
    "messages": "Login successful",
    "token": "<jwt>",
    "cookie": "<session_cookie_value>"
  }
}

Set-Cookie: sid=<value>; ... is set by SessionClass::register() at the transport edge.

Errors:

domainCodeHTTPCondition
AUTH.INVALID_CREDENTIALS409email/password empty, or no matching user row
AUTH.ACCOUNT_NOT_ACTIVE422user verified_email === 0 or is_active === 0
(fallback \Throwable)dynamicResponseEnvelopeHelper::statusCodeFromThrowable()

Usage:

curl -X POST https://api.gothru.co/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "your_username", "password": "your_password"}'

Notes:

  • email field maps to users.user_name column — not the user's email address.
  • Session lifetime hardcoded to 86400s (24h) in LoginController::SESSION_LIFETIME.
  • SessionClass::start()/register()/getCookie() intentionally retained at Controller (transport-edge HTTP concern), same pattern as LogoutController.
  • GT token (auth.token_id) is upserted on every successful login — previous token is invalidated.
  • Compliance: ~95% ADR. SessionClass retained at transport edge (acceptable); AuthTokenHelper is a pure static helper with zero legacy dependency.
